items.prompt: Example 10.12 primarily illustrates synthetic data for:
items.explain: Example 10.12 describes synthetic EHR tables that preserve cohort statistics without raw patient files.

items.prompt: Rare-disease synthesis helps when real cases are too few to train robust models.
items.explain: Sections 10.6.2 and Example 10.13 address low-prevalence conditions with synthetic augmentation.

items.prompt: Why is synthetic fraud data valuable in finance (Example 10.14)?
choices: Fraudulent activity is abundant and easy to log
Fraud is rare in historical data, so synthetic patterns expand positive training examples
Synthetic data removes all need for real transaction monitoring
items.explain: Section 10.6.3 and Example 10.14 note scarcity of real fraud cases and synthetic augmentation of patterns.

items.prompt: What must still be validated when sharing synthetic healthcare tables externally?
items.rubric: Mention re-identification risk, statistical fidelity, or regulatory compliance—not assuming privacy by label alone.
items.sample_answer: Teams must validate that synthetic tables preserve useful statistics while keeping re-identification risk acceptably low under HIPAA or GDPR.

items.prompt: Synthetic trading simulations can create market scenarios absent from limited historical data.
items.explain: Section 10.6.3 describes synthetic market data for crashes, booms, and edge cases not in historical windows.
